tables.title: Common Pediatric Toxidromes
rows: Altered mental status, pinpoint pupils, respiratory depression
Altered mental status, dilated pupils, dry flushed skin, tachycardia
Salivation, lacrimation, urination, defecation, GI upset, bradycardia
Agitation, tachycardia, hypertension, diaphoresis, dilated pupils

tables.title: Pediatric Heat Illness vs Hypothermia — Key Field Differences
rows: Hot, flushed (or pale/clammy in heat exhaustion)
May be altered in severe heat stroke
May be altered in severe hypothermia
Hot vehicle, high ambient temperature, limited sweating
Remove from heat, active cooling, ABC support
Remove from cold, gentle rewarming, ABC support
